intro: Copy any prompt below into the chat — or click 'Insert' to drop it into the input. Tweak the placeholders in <angle brackets> to match your sheet, then send. Tip: combine prompts from different categories for richer workflows.

difficulty.easy: Single tool call, no setup needed.
difficulty.medium: Multiple steps; the agent will likely ask for confirmation.
difficulty.advanced: Multi-sheet, formulas, or batch operations. Read the agent's plan carefully.

risk.caution: Modifies data; reversible from Smartsheet history if needed.
risk.destructive: Deletes data, removes shares, or changes structure permanently.

categories.title: Exploration & diagnostic
categories.description: Understand what's in a sheet without changing anything. Always a safe first step.

prompts.title: Quick overview of the current sheet
prompts.description: Get the structure (columns, types) plus the first rows in one shot.
prompts.prompt: Give me a quick overview of this sheet: total rows, all columns with their types, and the first 5 rows of data. End with anything that looks unusual (empty columns, mixed types, suspicious values).

prompts.title: Find empty or incomplete rows
prompts.description: Surface rows where critical columns are blank.
prompts.prompt: Scan the first 200 rows of this sheet and list those where the column <Status> is empty. For each, give me the row number and the value of the <Task> column.

prompts.title: Detect duplicate values
prompts.description: Spot rows that share the same value in a key column.
prompts.prompt: Look at the column <Order Number>. List every value that appears in more than one row, and for each duplicate show me the row numbers concerned. Don't change anything.

prompts.title: Summary by group
prompts.description: Aggregate counts (or sums) by a category column.
prompts.prompt: Group the rows by the column <Status> and tell me, for each value: how many rows it has, and the sum of the column <Quantity>. Format the answer as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: Full-text search across the sheet
prompts.description: Find every row containing a specific phrase, anywhere.
prompts.prompt: Search this sheet for the text '<keyword>'. List every row that contains it (any column), with the row number, the matching column, and the surrounding cell value. Cap the result at 50 hits.

prompts.title: Descriptive stats on a numeric column
prompts.description: Min / max / mean / median / sum on one column.
prompts.prompt: On the column <Amount>, compute and show: count of non-empty values, min, max, mean (rounded to 2 decimals), median, sum. Skip empty cells. Format as a small Markdown table.

prompts.title: Top N rows by a column
prompts.description: Find the highest (or lowest) values without writing a formula.
prompts.prompt: Show me the <10> rows with the highest <Amount> in this sheet. For each, include the row number and the columns <Customer> and <Date>. Sort descending by <Amount>.

prompts.title: Audit trail of a single cell
prompts.description: Who changed what, when, on a specific cell.
prompts.prompt: Pull the cell history for the column <Status> on row <row_number>. Show every revision: timestamp, author, previous value, new value. Format as a chronological Markdown table.

prompts.title: What did I touch recently?
prompts.description: List the sheets / reports the connected user opened last.
prompts.prompt: List the items I (the connected user) have accessed most recently across my Smartsheet account. For each: name, type (sheet/report/dashboard), workspace, and last-accessed date if available.

categories.title: Rows — data manipulation
categories.description: Add, update, move, copy, delete rows. The agent will ask for confirmation before each write.

prompts.title: Add one row
prompts.description: Insert a single row with explicit values.
prompts.prompt: Add one row to this sheet with: <Task>='Prepare report', <Status>='Not started', <Due Date>='2026-05-15'. Show me the payload before sending it.

prompts.title: Add several rows at once
prompts.description: Bulk insert from a list you paste.
prompts.prompt:
Add the following rows to this sheet. Use the columns <Task> and <Owner>:
1. Audit Q2 budget — Alice
2. Refresh dashboards — Bob
3. Plan kickoff — Carol

Confirm the planned payload before writing.

prompts.title: Update rows matching a condition
prompts.description: Change a value only on rows that match a filter.
prompts.prompt: For every row where the column <Status> = 'In progress' AND the column <Owner> is empty, set <Owner> = 'Unassigned'. List the affected row numbers BEFORE applying, then ask me to confirm.

prompts.title: Delete rows (with dry-run preview)
prompts.description: Always preview which rows will be deleted before pulling the trigger.
prompts.prompt: I want to delete every row where the column <Status> = 'Cancelled'. Don't delete anything yet — just list the row numbers, the values of <Task> and <Owner>, and the total count. I'll confirm explicitly.

prompts.title: Reorder a row
prompts.description: Move a row to a new position (top, bottom, before/after another row).
prompts.prompt: Move the row whose <Task> = '<task name>' to the top of the sheet. Confirm what you are about to do before applying.

prompts.title: Copy rows to another sheet
prompts.description: Duplicate rows into a target sheet (the source rows stay).
prompts.prompt: Copy every row where <Status> = 'Done' into the sheet '<target sheet>' (id=<target_sheet_id>). Preserve cell values; do NOT delete the source rows. List the count and the first 5 row numbers before applying, then wait for my confirmation.

prompts.title: Sort the sheet by a column
prompts.description: Persistent server-side sort (saved on the sheet itself).
prompts.prompt: Sort this sheet by <Due Date> ascending, then by <Priority> descending. Confirm the sort spec before applying.

prompts.title: Live link a cell to another sheet
prompts.description: Create a Smartsheet cell-link (the value updates when the source changes).
prompts.prompt: On row <row_number>, column <Customer Name> of THIS sheet, create a cell-link pointing to row <source_row_number>, column <Customer Name> of sheet '<source sheet>' (id=<source_sheet_id>). Confirm both endpoints before creating the link.

prompts.title: Add a comment to a row
prompts.description: Drop a discussion message on a specific row (visible to collaborators).
prompts.prompt: Add the comment '<your message>' on row <row_number>. Show me the row context (key columns) before posting so I'm sure it's the right one.

categories.title: Columns & structure
categories.description: Add, rename, retype, reorder, or remove columns. Be careful: changing a column type can lose data.

prompts.title: Add a TEXT_NUMBER column
prompts.description: Most common case: a free-text/number column at a chosen position.
prompts.prompt: Add a new column to this sheet titled 'Notes', type TEXT_NUMBER, placed at the very end. Don't add any rows.

prompts.title: Add a PICKLIST (dropdown) column
prompts.description: Restrict cell values to a fixed list of options.
prompts.prompt: Add a new PICKLIST column titled 'Priority' with the options: Low, Medium, High, Critical. Place it right after the <Status> column. Do not enable multi-select.

prompts.title: Add a multi-select PICKLIST column
prompts.description: Same as PICKLIST but cells can hold several values at once.
prompts.prompt: Add a multi-select PICKLIST column titled 'Tags' with options: backend, frontend, infra, design, docs. Multi-select MUST be enabled. Place it at the end.

prompts.title: Add a CHECKBOX column
prompts.description: Boolean flag column.
prompts.prompt: Add a CHECKBOX column titled 'Validated' at the end of the sheet.

prompts.title: Add a DATE column
prompts.description: Pure date column (no time component).
prompts.prompt: Add a DATE column titled 'Due Date' right after the column <Status>. Do not seed any default value.

prompts.title: Add a CONTACT_LIST column
prompts.description: Cell values are user contacts (with avatars).
prompts.prompt: Add a CONTACT_LIST column titled 'Owner' at the end of the sheet. Allow multiple contacts per cell.

prompts.title: Add a column with a default formula
prompts.description: All cells in the column will run the same formula.
prompts.prompt: Add a TEXT_NUMBER column titled 'Days open' at the end. Set its default formula to: =IF(ISBLANK([Created Date]@row), "", TODAY() - [Created Date]@row). Confirm the syntax with me before creating the column.

prompts.title: Rename a column
prompts.description: Pure metadata change, doesn't touch the data.
prompts.prompt: Rename the column currently called '<old name>' to '<new name>'. Don't change its type or position.

prompts.title: Reorder columns
prompts.description: Move a column to a new index.
prompts.prompt: Move the column '<column name>' to position <2> (zero-indexed). Show me the resulting column order before applying.

prompts.title: Delete a column (with audit)
prompts.description: Drops a column AND all its data — confirm twice.
prompts.prompt: I want to delete the column '<column name>'. First: tell me how many non-empty cells it contains and show me 3 sample values so I'm sure I'm deleting the right one. Then ask me for explicit confirmation. Only then perform the deletion.

categories.title: Cross-sheet — pull data from another sheet
categories.description: Bring values from a source sheet into the current one. The agent must create cross-sheet refs first, then write the formula.

prompts.title: VLOOKUP from another sheet
prompts.description: Look up a value in a source sheet based on a key column.
prompts.prompt:
I want to bring the value of the column <Customer Name> from the sheet '<source sheet name>' (id=<source_sheet_id>) into this sheet, joined on the column <Order Number>. Walk me through:
1. Read the source sheet schema and confirm the column ids.
2. Create the cross-sheet references you need.
3. Build the VLOOKUP formula.
4. Apply it on rows 1–<N> of column <Customer Name>.

Ask before each write.

prompts.title: INDEX / COLLECT from another sheet (more flexible than VLOOKUP)
prompts.description: Multi-criteria lookup that returns the first match — handles any column order.
prompts.prompt:
In this sheet, in the column <Status>, write a formula using INDEX and COLLECT that returns the value of <Status> from the sheet '<source sheet>' (id=<source_sheet_id>) for the row whose <Order Number> equals the <Order Number> of the current row AND whose <Customer> equals the <Customer> of the current row.

Mandatory steps:
1. Read the source schema, confirm the 3 column names.
2. Create the cross-sheet references (one per source column).
3. Show me the formula before applying.
4. Apply on rows 1–<N>.

prompts.title: One-shot transfer of values (no live link)
prompts.description: Copy values from a source sheet into the current sheet, in order. No formula, just static values.
prompts.prompt:
Source sheet id: <source_sheet_id>. Source column: '<source column>'. Target column in THIS sheet: '<target column>'. I want a one-shot transfer (static values, no formula), preserving the row order from the source.

Mandatory steps:
1. Confirm the exact source column name via get_sheet_summary.
2. Tell me how many non-empty rows the source has and how many rows the target has. Propose adding rows if the target is shorter.
3. Build the full mapping (target row → value to write) and show it as a Markdown table (cap display at 20 rows, mention the total).
4. WAIT for my explicit confirmation before any update_rows.

prompts.title: Aggregate from another sheet (SUMIFS / COUNTIFS)
prompts.description: Conditional sum or count using a cross-sheet range.
prompts.prompt: In this sheet, in the column <Total Amount>, write a formula that sums the column <Amount> from the sheet '<source sheet>' (id=<source_sheet_id>) for every row whose <Status> equals the value of <Status> in this row. Create the cross-sheet refs first.

prompts.title: Audit existing cross-sheet references
prompts.description: Inventory the references already defined on this sheet.
prompts.prompt: List every cross-sheet reference defined on this sheet. For each, show: ref name, target sheet (with id), target range (cell or full column), and where it is used (which formula / column). Format as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: Find values in this sheet missing from a source
prompts.description: Detect rows whose join key has no match in a master sheet.
prompts.prompt: I want to find rows in THIS sheet whose <Order Number> does NOT exist in the sheet '<master sheet>' (id=<master_sheet_id>). For each unmatched row, list: row number, <Order Number>, <Customer>. Don't write anything to the sheet — just report. You may build a temporary IFERROR(MATCH(...)) check if needed, then revert it after the audit.

categories.title: Formulas (single-sheet)
categories.description: Smartsheet-native formulas. Reminder: AVG (not AVERAGE), no AVERAGEIFS — use SUMIFS/COUNTIFS or AVG(COLLECT(...)).

prompts.title: Sum if a condition matches
prompts.description: Classic SUMIF on the same sheet.
prompts.prompt: In the cell <column=Total>, <row=last>, write a formula that sums the column <Amount> only for rows where <Status> = 'Paid'. Use SUMIF.

prompts.title: Count with multiple criteria
prompts.description: COUNTIFS to count rows that satisfy several conditions at once.
prompts.prompt: Add a new TEXT_NUMBER column titled 'High-prio open' and fill its first cell with a COUNTIFS formula counting rows where <Priority> = 'High' AND <Status> <> 'Done'.

prompts.title: Nested IF (status to label)
prompts.description: Translate a value to a human-readable label.
prompts.prompt:
In the column <Health>, write a formula that returns:
- 'On track' if <Status> = 'In progress' AND <Due Date> >= TODAY()
- 'At risk' if <Status> = 'In progress' AND <Due Date> < TODAY()
- 'Done' if <Status> = 'Done'
- empty otherwise.
Apply it to every row that already has data.

prompts.title: Age in days from today
prompts.description: Compute how many days have passed since a date.
prompts.prompt: In the column <Age (days)>, write a formula that returns TODAY() - <Created Date> (rounded to an integer). Skip rows where <Created Date> is empty.

prompts.title: Extract a substring (LEFT / RIGHT / MID / FIND)
prompts.description: Pull part of a text value into its own column.
prompts.prompt: In the column <Domain>, write a formula that extracts the domain part of the column <Email> (everything after '@'). Use FIND and MID. If <Email> is empty, return an empty string. Apply on every existing row.

prompts.title: Wrap a fragile formula with IFERROR
prompts.description: Replace #INVALID / #DIVIDE BY ZERO / #NO MATCH with a clean fallback.
prompts.prompt: In the column <Margin>, the formula =[Revenue]@row / [Cost]@row produces #DIVIDE BY ZERO when Cost is 0. Rewrite it as IFERROR(..., 0) so the result is 0 instead of an error. Apply to every row already having a value.

prompts.title: Business-days delta (NETWORKDAYS)
prompts.description: Count working days between two dates, ignoring weekends.
prompts.prompt: Add a TEXT_NUMBER column titled 'Working days to deadline' and write a formula =NETWORKDAYS(TODAY(), [Due Date]@row). If <Due Date> is empty, return an empty string.

prompts.title: Roll-up children to a parent row
prompts.description: Use SUM(CHILDREN()) to aggregate child rows on a parent.
prompts.prompt: In the column <Total>, on every parent row (row that has at least one indented child), write the formula =SUM(CHILDREN()). Tell me how many parent rows you found before applying.

prompts.title: Rank rows by a column
prompts.description: RANK to compute a rank position per row.
prompts.prompt: Add a TEXT_NUMBER column titled 'Rank' and fill it with =RANK([Score]@row, [Score]:[Score], 0) so that the highest score is rank 1. Apply to every row that has a value in <Score>.

prompts.title: Weighted percent complete
prompts.description: Combine multiple progress columns weighted by effort.
prompts.prompt: On the parent row <row_number>, write a formula that returns the weighted average of <% complete> across CHILDREN(), weighted by <Effort (days)>. Use SUMPRODUCT-style logic with SUM. Confirm the formula syntax against Smartsheet docs before writing it.

categories.title: Hierarchy & sub-tasks
categories.description: Smartsheet's killer feature: parent rows with indented children. Use for project plans, WBS, nested categories.

prompts.title: Print the indented structure
prompts.description: See parents and their children as an indented tree.
prompts.prompt: Read the first 200 rows and show the indentation tree: for each row, print '  ' × (level) + <Task>. Mark rows that have children with '▶'. Don't change anything.

prompts.title: Find all parent rows
prompts.description: List rows that have at least one child.
prompts.prompt: Identify every parent row (a row that has at least one indented child). For each, give me the row number, <Task>, and the count of direct children. Format as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: Add a child row under a parent
prompts.description: Insert a new row indented under an existing one.
prompts.prompt: Add a child row under the parent whose <Task> = '<parent task>'. Set <Task>='<child task>', <Owner>='<owner>'. Confirm the parent row id and the planned payload before writing.

prompts.title: Roll up children sum on every parent
prompts.description: Compute SUM(CHILDREN()) on every parent of a column.
prompts.prompt: On the column <Effort>, fill every parent row with =SUM(CHILDREN()). Leave child rows untouched. Tell me how many parent rows will be updated, then ask before applying.

prompts.title: Find childless leaves (audit)
prompts.description: Spot leaf rows that should logically have sub-tasks.
prompts.prompt: List every leaf row (no children) where <Type> = 'Epic' or <Type> = 'Phase'. Those probably should have sub-tasks. Just report — don't change anything.

categories.title: Sharing & permissions
categories.description: Invite or remove collaborators on the sheet (or its workspace). Always confirm with me before changing permissions.

prompts.title: List who has access
prompts.description: Read-only audit of current shares.
prompts.prompt: List every user and group currently shared on this sheet, with their access level (VIEWER, EDITOR, ADMIN, OWNER). Format as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: Invite a user
prompts.description: Add one or several users at a chosen level.
prompts.prompt: Share this sheet with <user@example.com> as EDITOR. Don't send the notification email. Confirm the action before applying.

prompts.title: Invite several users at once
prompts.description: Bulk share with the same access level.
prompts.prompt:
Share this sheet with the following users as VIEWER (no notification email):
- alice@example.com
- bob@example.com
- carol@example.com

List the planned shares and ask for confirmation before applying.

prompts.title: Change a user's access level
prompts.description: Promote / demote an existing collaborator.
prompts.prompt: For <user@example.com>, change the access level on this sheet from <current level> to <new level> (one of VIEWER, EDITOR, ADMIN). Confirm the current level first.

prompts.title: Revoke a user's access
prompts.description: Remove a collaborator from the sheet.
prompts.prompt: Remove <user@example.com> from this sheet's shares. List the current access level first so I can confirm we're removing the right person.

categories.title: Automations & alerts
categories.description: Inspect, pause, document, or remove automation rules attached to the sheet (notifications, update requests, approvals, locks).

prompts.title: List all automation rules
prompts.description: Audit what automations exist on the sheet.
prompts.prompt: List every automation rule on this sheet. For each, give me: name, trigger type (event-based / time-based), action type (notification / update / move / approval), and whether it is enabled. Format as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: Inspect one automation rule in depth
prompts.description: Get the full configuration of a rule.
prompts.prompt: Give me the full configuration of the automation rule named '<rule name>' on this sheet: trigger details, conditions, recipients, action details, schedule. Format as a structured Markdown block.

prompts.title: Document all rules in a Markdown report
prompts.description: Build a one-page audit doc describing every rule.
prompts.prompt: Produce a Markdown report titled '## Automation rules on <this sheet name>' describing every rule of this sheet. For each, include: name, status (enabled/disabled), trigger, conditions, action(s), recipients. End with a small summary: total count, % enabled, most-used action type.

prompts.title: Disable an automation
prompts.description: Pause a rule without deleting it.
prompts.prompt: Disable the automation rule named '<rule name>' on this sheet. Confirm what it does before disabling, and tell me how to re-enable it later.

prompts.title: Delete an automation
prompts.description: Permanently remove a rule.
prompts.prompt: Delete the automation rule named '<rule name>'. Show me its full configuration first, then ask for explicit confirmation.

categories.title: Discussions & comments
categories.description: Inspect conversation threads on a sheet, drop a comment on a row, or open a new discussion thread.

prompts.title: List all discussion threads on the sheet
prompts.description: Audit every conversation attached to the sheet itself or its rows.
prompts.prompt: List every discussion thread on this sheet. For each: thread id, attached row (or 'sheet-level'), title/first comment, author, date, number of replies. Format as a Markdown table sorted by most recent.

prompts.title: Read all comments on one row
prompts.description: See the full thread on a specific row.
prompts.prompt: Pull the full discussion thread on row <row_number>. Show every comment in chronological order with: timestamp, author, text. Just read — don't post anything.

prompts.title: Add a comment to a row's existing thread
prompts.description: Append a message to a row's discussion (or start one if none exists).
prompts.prompt: On row <row_number>, post the comment: '<your message>'. If the row has no existing thread, create a new one. Show me the row's key columns before posting so I can confirm I'm targeting the right row.

prompts.title: Open a new discussion on a row
prompts.description: Explicitly create a new thread (e.g. for a code review or blocker).
prompts.prompt: Open a new discussion thread on row <row_number> with the opening message: '<message>'. Confirm the row context first (key columns), then create the thread.

categories.title: Attachments
categories.description: Inspect and manage URL attachments on the sheet or on individual rows.

prompts.title: List all attachments on the sheet
prompts.description: Inventory every file or URL attached to the sheet or its rows.
prompts.prompt: List every attachment on this sheet. For each: name, type (file / link), attached to (sheet / row #), size if file, uploaded by, uploaded at. Sort newest first. Cap at 100 results.

prompts.title: List attachments on one row
prompts.description: See the files / links attached to a specific row.
prompts.prompt: List the attachments on row <row_number>. For each: name, type, URL or download link, uploaded by.

prompts.title: Attach a URL to a row
prompts.description: Add a clickable URL on a row (or on the sheet itself).
prompts.prompt: Attach the URL '<https://...>' (display name '<short label>') to row <row_number>. Confirm the row's key columns before attaching.

prompts.title: Audit & remove very old attachments
prompts.description: Find attachments older than X days and propose deletion.
prompts.prompt: List every attachment on this sheet that was uploaded more than <365> days ago. For each: name, attached to (row # or sheet), uploaded at, uploaded by. Don't delete anything yet — wait for me to pick which ones to drop. After my explicit list, delete only the chosen attachments.

categories.title: Reports & dashboards
categories.description: Read-only inspection of Smartsheet reports and dashboards visible to the connected user.

prompts.title: List all reports I can see
prompts.description: Inventory of reports across workspaces.
prompts.prompt: List every report I have access to. For each: name, id, workspace, last modified date. Sort by last modified desc. Cap at 50.

prompts.title: Inspect a report
prompts.description: Read a report's columns and the first rows.
prompts.prompt: Read the report id=<report_id>. Show its columns (with types), the source sheets it aggregates, and the first 10 rows. Format the rows as a Markdown table.

prompts.title: List all dashboards I can see
prompts.description: Inventory of dashboards (Sights).
prompts.prompt: List every dashboard (Sight) I have access to. For each: name, id, workspace, last modified date. Sort by last modified desc.

categories.title: Workspaces, folders & sheets
categories.description: Navigate and reorganise your Smartsheet account: list workspaces, create folders, copy / move / rename sheets.

prompts.title: List all my workspaces
prompts.description: Top-level view of every workspace I can access.
prompts.prompt: List every workspace I have access to. For each: name, id, my access level. Format as a Markdown table sorted alphabetically.

prompts.title: Print a workspace tree
prompts.description: Show all folders, sheets, reports, dashboards inside a workspace.
prompts.prompt: For workspace id=<workspace_id>, print an indented tree showing every folder, sheet, report and dashboard inside (recurse into folders). Mark each item with its type ([sheet], [report], [dashboard], [folder]).

prompts.title: Create a folder in a workspace
prompts.description: Organise sheets by creating a folder.
prompts.prompt: Create a folder named '<folder name>' inside workspace id=<workspace_id>. Confirm the parent workspace name before creating.

prompts.title: Copy a sheet to another workspace / folder
prompts.description: Duplicate a sheet (structure + data) to a new location.
prompts.prompt: Copy the sheet id=<source_sheet_id> into workspace id=<target_workspace_id> (folder id=<target_folder_id> if applicable), naming the copy '<new name>'. Include data, formulas, attachments, discussions if possible. Confirm the copy plan before executing.

prompts.title: Rename a sheet
prompts.description: Change the sheet's display name (no data change).
prompts.prompt: Rename the sheet id=<sheet_id> from its current name to '<new name>'. Show me the current name first to confirm I'm targeting the right one.

categories.title: Maintenance & quality
categories.description: Clean up, normalise, and fix data quality issues. Always preview before mutating.

prompts.title: Trim leading/trailing whitespace
prompts.description: Clean text columns that have extra spaces.
prompts.prompt: In the column <Customer Name>, find every cell whose value differs from its trimmed version (leading or trailing whitespace). List the affected row numbers. After my confirmation, update each cell to the trimmed value.

prompts.title: Normalise status values
prompts.description: Map free-text status to a canonical list.
prompts.prompt: List every distinct value of the column <Status> with its row count. Then propose a mapping to this canonical list: ['Not started', 'In progress', 'Blocked', 'Done', 'Cancelled']. Apply the mapping ONLY after I confirm.

prompts.title: Fill blank cells with a default
prompts.description: Set a default value on every empty cell of a column.
prompts.prompt: On the column <Priority>, find every row where the cell is empty AND the row is not entirely empty. Set those cells to '<Medium>'. Show me the count and 5 sample row numbers before applying.

prompts.title: Deduplicate rows on a key column
prompts.description: Keep one row per unique key, drop the rest (with preview).
prompts.prompt: Find duplicate rows in this sheet based on the key column <Order Number>. For each duplicate group, keep the row with the most recent <Last Modified> date and delete the others. Don't delete anything yet — list the row numbers to keep vs. to drop, total counts, and ask for explicit confirmation before applying.

prompts.title: Detect inconsistent date formats
prompts.description: Spot rows where a DATE column has unparseable values.
prompts.prompt: On the column <Date>, list every row where the value is non-empty but does not look like a valid ISO date (yyyy-mm-dd). For each, show: row number, raw value, suspected reason (text instead of date, wrong format, …). Just report — don't change anything.

prompts.title: Archive old rows to another sheet
prompts.description: Move (not copy) rows older than X to an archive sheet.
prompts.prompt: Move every row where <Done Date> is more than 180 days ago to the sheet '<archive sheet>' (id=<archive_sheet_id>), preserving column values. Then delete those rows from this sheet. Show me the count and the first 5 row numbers before doing anything.
